200.full_name: 'Umayr bin Sa'd bin 'Ubaid b. Nu'man b. Qays b. 'Amr b. Zayd b. Umayya b. Zayd b. Malik b. 'Auf
200.arabic: عمير بن سعيد بن عبيد الأنصاري
200.bio: A destitute boy from Madinah who became a Muslim at the age of ten.  Later when appointed by Caliph Umar as Governor of the town of Hims in Syria, he was renowned for his frugal lifestyle and just rule: Islam will remain strong so long as the Sultan or central authority is strong. The strength of the Sultan neither comes from flogging with the whip, nor killing with the sword but from ruling with justice and holding fast to truth.

201.full_name: Basbas bin 'Amr bin Tha'laba b. Kharasha b. Zayd b. 'Amr b. Sa'd of B. Juhayna

202.full_name: Umm Ayman Barakah bint Tha'labah bin 'Amr bin Hassan b. Maalik b. Salamah b. 'Amr b. Nu'man
202.arabic: أم أيمن مولاة النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم
202.bio: O Barakah, I shall depart from this world shortly. I commend my son Muhammad to your care. He lost his father while he was in my abdomen. Here he is now, losing his mother under his very eyes. Be a mother to him, Barakah. And don't ever leave him.'. The touching story of the cradle to grave devotion of  the Ethiopian slave girl who reared Prophet Mohammed after the death of his parents and was a selfless and courageous early Muslim.

203.full_name: Habiba bint Kharijah ibn Zayd b. Abi Zuhair b. Malik b. Imru'ul-Qays b. Malik b. Tha'laba b. Ka'b

204.full_name: 'Abdullah ibn Hudhaifa b. Qays b. 'Adi b. Sa'd b. Sahm b. 'Amr b. Husays b. Ka'b b. Lu'ayy
204.bio: A companion who met two great world leaders of the day.  First he was Prophet Mohammed's emissary to Khusraw Parvez, the King of Persia.  Years later during the caliphate of Umar ibn al-Khattab,  Abdullah ibn Hudhafah as-Sahmi was captured in battle against the Byzantine empire.  Neither inducements nor torture at the hands of the Emperor Heraclius could persuade him to renounce Islam.

205.arabic: الطفيل بن عبد الله بن الحارث بن سخبرة
205.bio: His mother, Umm Ruman, married a man from her tribe named 'Abdullah ibn Harith bin Sakhbarah Azdi. They moved to Mecca, where his father became the partner and companion of Abu Bakr. Soon after this his father died, and he and his widowed mother were left with no support. Seeing their precarious condition Abu Bakr married his mother.

206.full_name: 'Utbah ibn Ghazwan b. jabir b. Wahb b. Nusyab b. Malik b. al-Harith b. Maazin b. Mansur b. Ikrima
206.bio: Great Muslim military commander under Caliph Umar.  Founder of the city of Basra, he continued to live in a tent so as to strengthen his faith and not be seduced by soft living.The brotherhood was made b/w him and Abu Dajjana.

207.full_name: Mahabba bint ar-Rabi' ibn 'Amr bin Abi Zuhair b. Malik b. Imru'ul-Qays b. Malik b. Tha'laba b. Ka'b
207.arabic: محبة بنت الربيع بن عمرو بن أبي زهير

208.full_name: Muhammad bin 'Abdur Rahman ibn Abi Bakr b. 'Uthman b. 'Amir b. 'Amr b. Ka'b b. Sa'd b. Taym b. Murra
208.arabic: محمد بن عبد الرحمن بن أبي بكر

209.full_name: Umm Haram bint Milhan b. Khalid b. Zayd b. Haraam b. Jundub b. 'Amir b. Ghanm b. 'Ady b. al-Najjar
209.father_name: Malik b. Khalid b. Zaid b. Haraam
209.mother_name: Muliakah bint Malik bin 'Adi b. Zaid Manat

210.full_name: Huzayla bint 'Utba b. 'Amr b. Khadij b. 'Amir b. Jusham b. al-Harith b.al-Khazraj
210.mother_name: Umayma bint Suhaym of B.Malik B.al-Najjar

211.full_name: Zayd ibn Kharija ibn Zayd b. Abi Zuhair b. Malik b. Imru'ul-Qays b. Malik b. Tha'laba b. Ka'b
211.bio: He spoke after his death in the time of 'Uthman ibn 'Affan (ra).

212.full_name: 'Adi ibn Hatim bin 'Abdullah b. Sa'd b. al-Hashraj b. Imru'l Qays b. 'Adi b. Akhrum al-Tayyi'
212.bio: This Christian Arab king, for twenty years an inveterate enemy of Islam, went unprotected to Madinah to meet Muhammad(ﷺ) after the Prophet treated his captured sister kindly and released her. He lived for 120 years.

213.full_name: 'Abdullah/'Amr ibn Qays ibn Za'ida b. al-Asam b. Rawhah b. Hajr b. 'abd b. Ma'is b. 'Amir b. Lu'ayy
213.arabic: عبد الله بن أم مكتوم الأعمى القرشي
213.bio:
A blind, young cousin of Khadijah (raa) (from mother side), Abdullah ibn Umm Maktum was one of the earliest Muslims.
Abdullah witnessed the rise of Islam in Makkah. He was amongst the first to accept Islam. He lived through the persecution of the Muslims and suffered what the other companions of the Prophet experienced. His attitude, like theirs, was one of firmness, staunch resistance and sacrifice. Neither his dedication nor his faith weakened against the violence of the Quraysh onslaught. In fact, all this only increased his determination to hold on to the religion of God and his devotion to His messenger.
The sixteen verses of Surah Abasa were revealed to the noble Prophet about Abdullah ibn Umm Maktum, which have continued to be recited from that time till today and shall continue to be recited.
He was left in charge of Madinah on more than ten occasions during Prophet Muhammed's absences and later, despite his disability, went into battle as the standard bearer of the Muslims. Place me between two rows and give me the standard. I will carry it for you and protect it, for I am blind and cannot run away.""

214.full_name: Jamila bint Sa'd ibn ar-Rabi' bin 'Amr bin Abi Zubair b. Malik b. Imru'ul-Qays b. Malik b. Tha'laba
214.arabic: أم سعد/ أم خارجة/جميلة بنت سعد بن الربيع

215.full_name: 'Amra bint Hazm ibn Zayd b. Ludhan b. 'Amr b. 'Abd 'Auf b. Ghanm b. Malik b. Al-Najjar
215.mother_name: Khalida bint Anas of B. Sa'ida

216.full_name: al-Harith ibn Nawfal ibn al-Harith ibn 'Abd al-Muttalib b. Hashim b. 'Abd Manaf b. Quasyy b. Kilab
216.bio: He converted to Islam after the battle of Badr with his father. Appointed the Wali of Makkah.
216.spouses: 851,Umm 'Amr bint al-Muttalib b. Abi wad'a,Umm Walad

217.full_name: 'Umayr bin Wahb bin Khalaf b. Hudhafa b. Jumah b. 'Amr b. Husays b. Ka'b
217.mother_name: Umm Sakhila bint Hashim bin Sa'dy bin Sahm
217.bio: He went to Medina to assassinate Muhammad. He returned to Mecca as a Muslim missionary.

218.full_name: Yazid bin Abi Sufyan ibn Harb ibn Umayya b. 'Abd Shams b. 'Abd Manaf b. Qusayy b. Kilab

219.full_name: Abu 'Umair bin Zayd bin Sahl b. al-Aswad b. Haram b. 'Amr b. Zayd Manat b. 'Adi b. 'Amr b. Malik
219.arabic: أبو عمير بن أبي طلحة زيد بن سهل الأنصاري

220.full_name: Umama bint As-Samit b. Qays b. Asram b. Fihr b. Tha'laba b. Ghanm b. Salm b. 'Auf b. 'Amr
220.arabic: أمامة بنت الصامت بن قيس بن أصرم

221.full_name: Sohail bin Wahb b. Rabi'ah b. Hilal b. Malik b. Dhabah b. al-Harith b. Fihr

222.full_name: Umm Khultum bint Suhayl bin 'Amr b. 'Abd Shams b. 'Abd Wud b. Nasr b. Malik b. Hisl b. 'Amir
222.arabic: أم كلثوم بنت سهيل بن عمرو
222.mother_name: Fakhitah bint 'Amir b. Nawfal b. 'Abd Manaf

223.full_name: Abu Sibrah bin Abi Ruhm b. 'Abdul 'Uzza b. Abi Qays b. 'Abd Wud b. Nasr b. Malik b. Hisl b. 'Amir
223.arabic: أبو سبرة بن أبي رهم بن عبد العزي
223.father_name: Abi Ruhm b. 'Abdul 'Uzza b. Abi Qays

224.full_name: Fatima bint Qays bin Khalid b. Wahb b. Tha'laba b. Wa'il b. 'Amr  b. Shiban b. Muharib b. Fihr
224.father_name: Qays bin Khalid Akbar bin Wahb
224.bio:
It was said that in the year 10 AH, Abu Umar bin Hafs divorced Fatima irrevocably
when he was away from her on return from a trip to Yemen as his agent was
sent to give her some barley, but she exasperated him. Learning of this Abu
Umar replied, 'By God, you will have nothing form me!, and so the divorce.
She went to his family and asked them to provide her with lodging and
maintenance and they refused.  Later, on reccomendation of the Prophet, she
married Usama. Usama died in 54 AH and that Fatima never remarried, instead
she lived with her brother, Dahhak ibn Qays.
224.students: 10535,13389,10567,11002,10511,عبد الله بن عبد الله بن عبيد بن مسعود,11054,11005,10939,11218,11052,18631,تميم مولى فاطمة بنت قيس

225.full_name: Ad-Dahhak bin Qays b. Khalid Akbar b. Wahb b. Tha'laba b. Wa'ila b. 'Amr b. Shiban b. Muharib b.Fihr
225.father_name: Qays bin Khalid Akbar bin Wahb

226.full_name: Thabit bin As-Samit b. Qays b. Asram b. Fihr b. Qays b. Tha'laba b. Ghanm b. Salm b. 'Auf b. 'Amr
226.arabic: ثابت بن الصامت بن قيس بن أصرم

227.full_name: Nadla bin 'Ubaid/'Aidh/'Amr or Khalid/'Abdullah bin Nadla

228.full_name: Khawla bint As-Samit b. Qays b. Asram b. Fihr b. Qays b. Tha'laba b. Ghanm b. Salm b. 'Auf b. 'Amr
228.arabic: خولة بنت الصامت بن قيس بن أصرم

229.full_name: Zayd bin Sahl b. al-Aswad b. Haram b. 'Amr b. Zayd Manat b. 'Adi b. 'Amr b. Malik b. al-Najjar
229.arabic: أبو طلحة الأنصاري زيد بن سهل بن الأسود
229.bio: His conversion to Islam was the Mahr for his wife, Umm Sulaym.

230.full_name: Addas Client of Shayba bin Rabi'a
230.bio: Addas was a young Christian slave boy who lived in Taif, a mountainous area south of Mecca, during the times of Muhammad. He was the first person from the western province of Taif to convert to the new religion of Islam.

231.full_name: Arwa bint 'Abd al-Muttalib b. Hashim b. 'Abd Manaf b. Qusayy b. Kilab b. Murrah b. Ka'b b. Lu'ayy
231.arabic: أروى بنت عبد المطلب عمة رسول الله
231.spouses: 'Umayr ibn Wahb ibn 'Abdul Munaf,Arta ibn Sharhbil ibn Hashim

232.full_name: Ka'b bin Jimaz(Himar) bin Tha'laba b. Kharasha('Uthman) b. Zayd b. 'Amr b. Sa'd of B. Juhayna

233.full_name: Qatada ibn al-Nu'man b. Zayd b. 'Amir b. Sawad b. Zafar b. al-Khazraj b. 'Amr b. Malik b. al-Aus
233.arabic: ذو العينين , قتادة بن النعمان بن زيد
233.bio:
Qatada ibn al-Nu'man was one of the Sahaba of Muhammad and a Ansar. He had a grandson named Asim ibn Umar ibn Qatada ibn al-Nu`man al-Ansari's (d. 120 or 129).
His eyes came out during battle of Uhud, but Prophet(ﷺ) put it back(miracle). ذو العينين
233.spouses: Hind bint Aws of B.Amr B.'Auf,Khansa bint Khunays Ghasai,or,660

234.full_name: 'Utba bin Rabai'a bin Khalid bin Mu'awiya b. Bahra, an ally from B.Ghusayna

235.full_name: Ma'mar bin Harith bin Ma'mar b. Habib b. Wahb b. Hudhafa b. Jumah b. 'Amr b. Husays b. Ka'b
235.father_name: Harith bin Ma'mar b. Habib b. Wahb b. Hudhafa
235.bio: The brotherhood was made b/w him and Mu'adh bin 'Afra(al-Harith). Participated in Major battles with Prophet (ﷺ).

236.full_name: 'Amr bin Iyyas bin Zayd b. Jushum, an ally from Yaman from Ghassan

237.full_name: Salma ibn ('Amr bin) al-Akwa(Sinan) b. 'Abdullah b. Qashieer b. Khuziama b. Malik b. Sinan b. Aslam
237.bio:
He is one of the notable narrators of hadith. He is known for being an excellent athlete and a very fast runner, and could even outrun horses.
سلمة بن عمرو بن الأكوع واسم الأكوع سنان بن عبد الله أبو عامر وأبو مسلم ويقال أبو إياس الأسلمي الحجازي المدني
سلمة بن الأكوع بن عبد الله بن قشير بن خزيمة بن مالك بن سلامان بن أسلم من خزاعة الأسلمى أبو عامر ويقال أبو إياس وقد قيل أبو مسلم

238.full_name: Suhayl ibn 'Amr b. 'Abd Shams b. 'Abd Wud b. Nasr b. Malik b. Hisl b. 'Amir b. Lu'ayy
238.bio:
Suhayl ibn Amr was a prominent man among the Quraish, being known as the khatib or orator of the tribe. Clever and Articulate, Suhayl personal opinion carried a heavy weight among his tribe. He was known to play a major role in concluding the famous truce of Hudaybiyyah. He died in Palestine in the small village of 'Amawas near Jerusalem.
When the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, passed away, the news quickly reached Makkah, where Suhayl was still resident. The Muslims were plunged into a state of confusion and dismay just as in Madinah. In Madinah, Abu Bakr, may God be pleased with him, quelled the confusion with his decisive words: Whoever worships Muhammad, Muhammad is dead. And whoever worships Allah, Allah is indeed Living and will never die."
In Makkah Suhayl performed the same role in dispelling the vain ideas some Muslims may have had and directing them to the eternal truths of Islam. He called the Muslims together and in his brilliant and salutary style, he affirmed to them that Muhammad was indeed the Messenger of Allah and that he did not die until he had discharged his trust and propagated the message and that it was the duty of all believers after his death to apply themselves assiduously to following his example and way of life.
"

239.full_name: Thabit bin Qays b. Shammas b. Zuhair b. Malik b. Imru'ul-Qays b. Malik b. Tha'laba b. Ka'b
239.arabic: ثابت بن قيس بن الشماس الأنصاري

240.full_name: 'Ady bin Qays bin Hudhafa b. Qays b. 'Adi b. Sa'd b. Sahm b. 'Amr b. Husays b. Ka'b b. Lu'ayy
240.arabic: عدي بن قيس بن حذافة السهمي

241.short_name: Umayma bint Ady bin Qays bin Hudhafa
241.full_name: Umayma bint 'Ady bin Qays bin Hudhafa b. Qays b. 'Adi b. Sa'd b. Sahm b. 'Amr b. Husays b. Ka'b
241.arabic: أميمة بنت عدي بن قيس بن حذافة السهمي

242.full_name: Wahshy ibn Harb al-Habshi, client of Jubayr bin Mut'im
242.bio: Wahshy ibn Harb was the Ethiopian slave of Jubayr ibn Mutim and a companion of Muhammad. He is best known for killing a leading Muslim general, Hamza ibn Abd al-Muttalib the Prophet Muhammad's uncle, prior to accepting Islam, and afterwards killing Musaylimah, the leader of an enemy apostate army who were waging war against the Muslims.

243.full_name: Yasir ibn 'Amir bin Malik b. Kananh b. Qyas al-Ansi
243.father_name: 'Amir bin Malik b. Kananh b. Qyas al-Ansi
243.bio: He left his native place in Yemen seeking a brother of his. In Makkah he found an appealing place, so he settled there and was in alliance with Abu Hudhaifah ibn al-Mughirah, who married him to one of his slave women, Sumayyah bint Khabbab. Out of this marriage they were granted a son, Ammar ibn Yasir. Their embracing of Islam was early. They had their good share of the Quraish's persecution and terror.

244.full_name: Zaynab bint Abi Salamah ibn 'Abd al-Asad bin Hilal b. 'Abdullah b. 'Umar b. Makhzum b. Yaqazah

245.full_name: Salamah bin Abi Salamah ibn 'Abd al-Asad bin Hilal b. 'Abdullah b. 'Umar b. Makhzum b. Yaqazah
245.arabic: سلمة بن أبي سلمة بن عبدالأسد

246.full_name: 'Umar bin Abi Salamah ibn 'Abd al-Asad bin Hilal b. 'Abdullah b. 'Umar b. Makhzum b. Yaqazah
246.arabic: عمر بن أبي سلمة بن عبد الأسد

247.full_name: Durrah bin Abi Salamah ibn 'Abd al-Asad bin Hilal b. 'Abdullah b. 'Umar b. Makhzum b. Yaqazah
247.arabic: درة بنت أبي سلمة بن عبدالأسد

248.full_name: Duba'a bint az-Zubair ibn 'Abd al-Muttalib b. Hashim b. 'Abd Manaf b. Qusayy b. Kilab b. Murrah
248.arabic: ضباعة بنت الزبير بن عبد المطلب الهاشمية

249.full_name: Umm al-Hakam bint az-Zubair ibn 'Abd al-Muttalib b. Hashim b. 'Abd Manaf b. Qusayy b. Kilab b. Murra
249.arabic: أم الحكم بنت الزبير بن عبد المطلب

250.full_name: Safiyya bint az-Zubair ibn 'Abd al-Muttalib b. Hashim b. 'Abd Manaf b. Qusayy b. Kilab b. Murrah
250.arabic: صفية بنت الزبير بن عبد المطلب بن هاشم

251.full_name: 'Itban bin Malik bin 'Amr b. al-'Ajlan b. Zayd b. Ghanm b. Salm b. 'Auf b. 'Amr b. 'Auf b. al-Khazra
251.arabic: عتبان بن مالك بن عمرو بن العجلان
251.bio:
The Brotherhood was b/w him and 'Umar bin al-Khattab.
After he became blind he said to the Prophet: I would like you to pray in my house so that I can pray where you prayed." The Prophet went to his house and asked where exactly he would like him to pray. He indicated a spot to him and the Prophet prayed there."

252.full_name: Layla bint Ri'ab/Rithab bin Hunayf b. Ziyad b. Umayya b. Zayd b. Ghanm b. Salm b. 'Auf b. 'Amr
252.spouses: 251,'Abdur Rahman ibn 'Amir of B.Waqif,'Abdullah bin 'Amr of B.Zafar

253.full_name: 'Asma bin Ri'ab/Rithab bin Hunayf b. Ziyad b. Umayya b. Zayd b. Ghanm b. Salm b. 'Auf b. 'Amr
253.bio: The above mentioned lineage seems more correct. Other lineage: 'Asma bin Ri'ab bin Hunayf b. Ri'ab b. al-Harith b. Umayya b. Zayd b. Ghanm b. Salm b. 'Auf b. 'Amr

254.full_name: Thabit bin Hazzal bin 'Amr b. Quryush b. Ghanm b. Umayya b. Laudhan b. Salm b. 'Auf b. 'Amr

255.full_name: 'Amra bint Hazzal bin 'Amr b. Quryush b. Ghanm b. Umayya b. Laudhan b. Salm b. 'Auf b. 'Amr

256.full_name: Abaan bin Sa'id ibn al-'As b. Umayya b. 'Abd Shams b. 'Abd Manaf b. Qusayy b. Kilab b. Murrah
256.arabic: أبان بن سعيد بن العاص بن أمية
256.mother_name: Umm Khalid bint Khabab b. 'Abd al-kanani

257.full_name: Umm az-Zubayr bint az-Zubair ibn 'Abd al-Muttalib b. Hashim b. 'Abd Manaf b. Qusayy b. Kilab b. Murr
257.arabic: أم الزبير بنت الزبير بن عبد المطلب

258.full_name: Rabi' bin Iyas b. 'Amr b. Ghanm b. Umayya b. Laudhan b. Salm b. 'Auf b. 'Amr b. 'Auf b.al-Khazraj
258.father_name: Iyas b. Amr b. Ghanm b. Umayya

259.full_name: Abd' bin Jahsh bin Ri'ab b. Ya'mar b. sabra b. Murra b. Kabir b. Ghanm b. Dudan b. Asad b. Khuziyah

260.full_name: Wazfah/Waraqa bin Iyas b. 'Amr b. Ghanm b. Umayya b. Laudhan b. Salm b. 'Auf b. 'Amr b. 'Auf
260.father_name: Iyas b. Amr b. Ghanm b. Umayya

261.full_name: 'Aatika bint 'Abd al-Muttalib b. Hashim b. 'Abd Manaf b. Qusayy b. Kilab b. Murrah
261.arabic: عاتكة بنت عبد المطلب عمة رسول الله
261.bio: Her conversion to Islam is not agreed upon.
261.spouses: Abu Umayyah b. al-Mughirah b. 'Abdullah Makhzumi

262.full_name: al-Mujadhar/'Abdullah bin Ziyad b. 'Amr b. Zumzuma b. 'Amr b.'Umara b. Malik of B. Quda'a
262.bio: Brotherhood was made b/w him and 'Aaqil bin abi al-Bukayr. He was buried with Nu'man bin Malik and 'Ubada bin al-Hashas at Uhud.

263.full_name: 'Ubada bin al-Hasha (or Khashkhash) b. 'Amr b. Zumzuma b. 'Amr b.'Umara b. Malik of B. Quda'a
263.bio: He was buried with Nu'man bin Malik and al-Mujhadhar bin Ziyad at Uhud.

264.full_name: Bahhath/Nahhab bin Tha'laba bin Hazama b. Asram b. 'Amr b.'Umara b. Malik of B. Quda'a

265.full_name: 'Abdullah bin Tha'laba bin Hazama b. Asram b. 'Amr b.'Umara b. Malik of B. Quda'a
265.arabic: عبد الله بن ثعلبة بن خزمة

266.full_name: Yazid bin Tha'laba bin Hazama b. Asram b. 'Amr b.'Umara b. Malik of B. Quda'a

267.full_name: Abu Fakih, Aflah bin Yasar client of Safwan bin Umayya
267.arabic: أبو فكيهة الجهمي مولى صفوان بن أمية
267.bio: He was tortured in scorching heat at noon and forced to lie naked, with a heavy stone on his chest. Once, the pagans tied a rope around his feet and neck and dragged about until he nearly died. Abu Bakr(ra) finally rescued him and freed him.

268.arabic: أفلح بن أبي القعيس،،و أخوأبي القعيس
268.bio: He was uncle of 'Aisha(ra) due to Suckling.

269.arabic: أفلح مولى رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم

271.full_name: Kharija ibn Zayd b. Abi Zuhair b. Malik b. Imru'ul-Qays b. Malik b. Tha'laba b. Ka'b
271.arabic: خارجة بن زيد بن أبى زهير
271.father_name: Zayd b. Abi Zuhair b. Malik
271.bio: Brotherhood was made between him and Abu Bakr Siddique (ra). He and Sa'd bin Rabi'(ra) were buried in same grave at Uhud.

272.full_name: 'Amra bint Rawaha b. Tha'laba b. Imru'ul-Qays b. 'Amr b. Imru'ul-Qays b. Malik b. Tha'laba b. Kab

273.full_name: Simak ibn Sa'd b. Tha'laba b. Jilas(Khilas) b. Zayd b. Malik b. Tha'laba b. Ka'b b. al-Khazraj
273.mother_name: Anisa bint Khalifa b. 'Ady of B.al-Harith

274.full_name: al-Nu'man bin Bashir ibn Sa'd b. Tha'labah b. Jilas(Khilas) b. Zayd b. Malik b. Tha'laba b. Ka'b
274.bio:
He was the first born in Medina from Ansar after migration.
He was Governor of Kufa.
He was also Qadi (Judge) of Damascus.

275.short_name: Umayya or Ubayya bint Bashir ibn Sa'd
275.full_name: Umayya/Ubayya bint Bashir ibn Sa'd b. Tha'labah b. Jilas(Khilas) b. Zayd b. Malik b. Tha'laba b. Kab
275.arabic: أميمة بنت بشير بن سعد الأنصارية

276.full_name: Khallad ibn Suwayd b. Tha'laba b. 'Amr b. Imru'ul-Qays b. Malik b. Tha'laba b. Ka'b b. al-Khazraj
276.arabic: خلاد بن سويد بن ثعلبة الأنصاري
276.mother_name: 'Amra bint Sa'd b. Qays if B.Harithah b.al-Khazraj
276.bio: Prophet(ﷺ) said that he will have reward for 2 Martyrs.

277.full_name: al-Saib bin Khallad ibn Suwayd b. Tha'laba b. 'Amr b. Imru'ul-Qays b. Malik b. Tha'laba b. Ka'b
277.arabic: السائب بن خلاد بن سويد الأنصاري
277.bio: He was governor of Yemen for 'Umar.

278.full_name: Mandwas bint Khallad ibn Suwayd b. Tha'laba b. 'Amr b. Imru'ul-Qays b. Malik b. Tha'laba b. Ka'b
278.arabic: مندوس بنت خلاد بن سويد الأنصاري

279.full_name: Unaysa or Nufaysa bint Qays b. Tha'laba b. Zayd b. al-Nu'man b. Malik b. Tha'laba b. Ka'b
279.arabic: أنيسة ويقال نفيسة بنت ثعلبة بن زيد

280.full_name: Subay' bin Qays bin 'Aysha(or 'Absa) b. Umayya b. Malik b. 'Amir b. 'Adiy b. Ka'b b. al-Khazraj
280.mother_name: Khadija bint 'Amr bin Zayd of B.al-Khazraj

281.full_name: 'Abbad bin Qays bin 'Aysha(or 'Absa) b. Umayya b. Malik b. 'Amir b. 'Adiy b. Ka'b b. al-Khazraj
281.mother_name: Khadija bint 'Amr bin Zayd of B.al-Khazraj

282.full_name: Yazid bin al-Harith ibn Qays b. Malik b. Ahmer b. Haritha b. Tha'laba b. Ka'b b. al-Khazraj
282.bio: Brotherhood was made b/w him and Dhu'l-Shimalyn 'Umair bin 'Abd 'Amr(id:828). Both Were Martyred at Badr.

283.full_name: Hurayth bin Zayd b. Tha'laba (b. 'Abdu Rabbihi) b. 'Abdullah b. Zayd b. al-Harith b.al-Khazraj

284.full_name: Sufyan bin Nasr bin 'Amr b. al-Harith b. Ka'b b. Zayd b. al-Harith b.al-Khazraj
284.arabic: سفيان بن نسر بن عمرو بن الحارث
284.bio: His name is also mentioned as Sufyan bin Bashr..

285.full_name: Khubayb bin Isaf(Yasaf) b. 'Itabah b. 'Amr b. Khadij b. 'Amir b. Jusham b. al-Harith b.al-Khazraj
285.bio: He became Muslim when Army was leaving for Badr.

286.full_name: Jamila bint 'Abdullah ibn Ubayy (b. Salul) b. Malik b. Harith b. 'Ubaid b. Malik  b. Salm b. Ghanm
286.arabic: جميلة بنت عبد الله بن أبي بن سلول
286.mother_name: Khawla bint al-Mundhir of Mailk b. al-Najjar
286.bio: Her Father was the infamous Hypocrite.

287.full_name: Zaynab bint Qays b. Shammas b. Zuhair b. Malik b. Imru'ul-Qays b. Malik b. Tha'laba b. Ka'b
287.arabic: زينب بنت قيس بن شماس الأنصارية

288.full_name: Unaysa bint Khubayb bin Isaf(Yasaf) b. 'Itabah b. 'Amr b. Khadij b. 'Amir b. Jusham

289.short_name: Umm Thabit bint Qays b. Shammas
289.full_name: Umm Thabit bint Qays b. Shammas b. Zuhair b. Malik b. Imru'ul-Qays b. Malik b. Tha'laba b. Ka'b
289.arabic: أم ثابت بنت قيس بن شماس الأنصارية

290.full_name: Kabsha bint Waqid bin 'Amr b. 'Amir b. Zayd Manat b. 'Amr
290.arabic: كبشة بنت واقد بن عمرو الأنصارية

291.short_name: Muhammad bin Thabit ibn Qays b. Shammas
291.full_name: Muhammad bin Thabit ibn Qays b. Shammas b. Zuhair b. Malik b. Imru'ul-Qays b. Malik b. Tha'laba
291.arabic: محمد بن ثابت بن قيس بن شماس الأنصاري
291.spouses: Umm 'Abdullah bint Hafs of B.Harith b.al-Khazraj,Others

292.short_name: Zaynab bint Thabit ibn Qays b. Shammas
292.full_name: Zaynab bint Thabit ibn Qays b. Shammas b. Zuhair b. Malik b. Imru'ul-Qays b. Malik b. Tha'laba
292.arabic: زينب بنت ثابت بن قيس بن شماس الأنصارية

293.short_name: 'Abdullah bin Thabit ibn Qays b. Shammas
293.full_name: 'Abdullah bin Thabit ibn Qays b. Shammas b. Zuhair b. Malik b. Imru'ul-Qays b. Malik b. Tha'laba
293.arabic: عبد الله بن ثابت بن قيس بن شماس الأنصاري

294.full_name: 'Abdur Rahman bin Thabit ibn Qays b. Shammas b. Zuhair b. Malik b. Imru'ul-Qays b. Malik b. Tha'laba
294.arabic: عبد الرحمن بن ثابت بن قيس الأنصاري

295.full_name: Yahya bin Thabit ibn Qays b. Shammas b. Zuhair b. Malik b. Imru'ul-Qays b. Malik b. Tha'laba
295.arabic: يحيى بن ثابت بن قيس بن شماس الأنصاري

296.full_name: Qurayba bint Zayd b. Tha'laba (b. 'Abdu Rabbihi) b. Zayd b. al-Harith b.al-Khazraj
296.arabic: قريبة بنت زيد بن عبد ربه الأنصارية

297.full_name: Tamim bin Ya'ar bin Qays b. 'Adi b. Umayya b. Jadara b. 'Auf b. al-Harith b. al-Khazraj
297.arabic: تميم بن يعار بن قيس بن عدي
297.spouses: A women from B. 'Amr b Waqsh

298.full_name: Yazid/Zayd bin al-Muzayyan bin Qays b. 'Adi b. Umayya b. Jidara b. 'Auf b. al-Harith b. al-Khazraj
298.arabic: يزيد بن المزين بن قيس بن عدي
298.bio: Brotherhood was made b/w him and Mistah bin Uthatha.

299.full_name: 'Abdullah bin 'Umair bin 'Adi (Haritha b. Tha'laba b. Jalas) b. Umayya b. Jidara(khidara) b. 'Auf
299.bio: There is some confusion over him, 'Abdullah bin 'Urfuta and 'Abdullah bin 'Abs.
